config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa pagar, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menindas orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat pribadi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati sambil tetap ada tanggung jawab sosial.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have blanket priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuse of minorities and government overreach. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be balanced against constitutional rights like free speech, due process, and equal protection, or else "society" becomes a vague excuse for power. I am more comfortable saying the public interest can limit individual rights in narrow, clearly justified cases, not that it should automatically come first. A healthy society protects people precisely by refusing to treat the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau mengorbankan orang kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar supaya tidak berubah jadi alat pembenaran penyalahgunaan wewenang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat adalah yang melindungi individu sambil tetap menuntut tanggung jawab sosial, bukan yang menjadikan individu sebagai korban demi label “demi masyarakat.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of language has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and overreach. I do think there are real cases where the public interest can override a specific liberty, like emergency health measures or public safety limits, but those exceptions need to be narrow, transparent, and legally accountable. What worries me most is when "society" is treated as a blank check, because then the people with the most power get to define the common good for everyone else. To me, a healthy society is one that protects the individual first and only limits rights when there is a clear, serious, and narrowly tailored reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis lebih utama daripada hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai untuk menormalisasi pembatasan yang terlalu jauh, padahal yang paling terdampak biasanya justru orang kecil, minoritas, atau mereka yang tidak punya akses kuasa. Bagi saya, kepentingan publik boleh membatasi hak individu hanya dalam kondisi yang sangat jelas, terukur, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sebagai cek kosong bagi negara atau mayoritas. Kalau hak individu tidak dijaga sebagai batas, “masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alasan yang rapi untuk menindas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement that society should take priority over the individual, because from an American perspective that usually sounds like a license for the majority or the state to crush dissent and minority rights. I do think the public interest can justify limits in narrow cases like emergencies or direct harm to others, but those limits have to be specific, accountable, and temporary. What I reject is the idea of a general hierarchy where "society" automatically outranks the person, because that turns human beings into means rather than ends. In the United States, our strongest protections come from treating individual rights as the default and forcing any exception to prove itself.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
